title: Pityrogramma ascensionis
intro: Pityrogramma ascensionis, also known as the Ascension fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Pteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pityrogramma ascensionis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pityrogramma ascensionis is often prepared as a tea or tincture, which is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Pityrogramma ascensionis is classified as a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. This plant is native to Central and South America. It is a type of epiphytic fern, which means it grows on other plants or objects for support. Pityrogramma ascensionis is also known for its unique, branching fronds that help it to thrive in its environment. The species is part of the genus Pityrogramma, which consists of several species of ferns.
